question: What are the four main principles of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)? Can you briefly explain each one?

scores.feedback: You correctly identified all four main principles of Object-Oriented Programming. To improve, focus on articulating your explanations more clearly and concisely. For encapsulation, ensure you clearly state it's about bundling data and methods together (data hiding and data binding). For polymorphism, elaborate slightly more on how objects can take 'many forms' (e.g., method overloading/overriding). Practicing these explanations aloud will help improve your fluency and confidence, ensuring smoother transitions and more precise language.

transcript: The main four principles of object oriented programming are encapsulation, abstraction, polymorphism and inheritance. Basically, the polymorphism means many forms where a single piece of code have different forms in polymorphism or in the encapsulation means the handless two things. One is data hiding and another is data bandiling with the data group as single class. Abstraction, it will hide the implementation and show only features. And in inheritance, we can get the features of the parent class to the child class.
